fields.description: Masyadong maikli yung skirt
pangalawa mo na to maam
Unnecessary shouting inside university buildings, hallways, or corridors
Loitering along hallways or corridors
Sitting on tables, stairs, veranda, and railings
Sliding on handrails of stairs
Use of vulgar or profane language
Disregarding environmental policies enforced by Campus Sustainability Office
Any similar misbehavior
See-through clothes without proper inner garments
Ripped jeans exposing skin 3 inches above the knee
Walking shorts more than 3 inches above the kneecap
Sleeveless blouses
Blouses and dresses with plunging neckline or backless design
Midriffs, hanging blouses, off-shoulder blouses and dresses
Skirts or dresses more than 2 inches above the knee
Overly tight clothing for male and female (including leggings and jeggings)
Haltered blouses/dresses and crop tops
Clothes (including caps) with offensive prints or statements
All kinds of slippers (except during heavy rains or injuries)
Improper or unauthorized use of classroom and equipment (LCD monitor, projector, board, TV, aircon)
Non-wearing of school ID
Violation of class policies and procedures stated in the syllabus
Exposed body piercing for males and females; earrings for males; more than one pair of earrings for females
Proselytizing
Posting of non-approved printed or online materials; unauthorized posting or removal of approved materials
Cross-dressing (unless approved by the Deans for specific activities)
Unauthorized changing of computer setup and default network setup
Unofficial use of download and chat tools or instant messengers through computer resource facility
Intentional posting or transmitting information or software containing virus, worm, or harmful features
Unauthorized use of routers
Unauthorized playing of web, network, or PC games in any computer resource facility
Sending of unofficial mass e-mail causing complaints to DLSU-D mailing list
Intentional sending of unsolicited e-mails with large attachments to DLSU-D mailing list
Copying large non-work-related files over the network
Unauthorized sharing of folders other than the default shared folder set by ICTC
Using university-owned IT devices as storage for mp3s and movies
Installing unlicensed software in university-owned IT devices
Usage of anonymous proxy to access regulated or restricted sites
Installation of rogue access points without consulting ICTC
Installation of non-ICTC unauthorized test servers
Cloning the University website or system to attempt phishing
Unauthorized access to any account or computer resource not belonging to the user
Selling of prohibited and/or regulated drugs
Drug addiction and/or possession or use of prohibited and regulated drugs (without valid prescription) within and outside university premises
Provocation in any form that results in heated verbal argument or physical confrontation between students

Acts of gross disrespect (in words or deeds) which insult or subject to ridicule any member of faculty, administration, staff, students, or visitors — on campus, off-campus, or through electronic means

Unjust vexation, discrimination, bullying (as defined by law); posting person-to-person damnation or cursing; ill wishing a person's state, ethnicity, cultural background, religion, or political beliefs; using bad and offending language; **includes cyberbullying**
Offering anything of value to a member of academic community in exchange for undue personal gain or as a favor for another
Acts of lewdness

Deliberate disruption of academic function or any university activity; any intentional act that creates disorder, tumult, breach of peace, or serious disturbance; includes entering a class or breaking into a university function without permission
Publishing misleading information in social media or in print
Forging or falsifying any official university document or record
Acts that malign the good name and reputation of the University, its authorities, and members of the academic community; includes malicious imputation of crime, offense, impropriety, vice, or defect
Unauthorized collection of money, checks, or other monetary instruments in connection with any matter pertaining to the University
Vandalism
Littering
Smoking and/or using e-cigarettes or e-vaporizers within university premises or approved off-campus activities
Gambling in any form inside the University premises and approved off-campus activities
Participating in approved off-campus activity while in a state of intoxication or under the influence of liquor
Possession of deadly weapon/s
Bringing, sending, or sharing of obscene, profane, or pornographic materials inside the University or during off-campus activities; through electronic means; or visiting pornographic sites
Entering or being in the University premises while in a state of intoxication or under the influence of liquor

Public display of affection: kissing, necking, petting, sitting/lying on somebody else's lap, partner or couple staying in dark inconspicuous places, any act constituting malicious public display of affection
Final conviction before any court of any criminal offense involving moral turpitude
Habitual commission of minor offenses (of the same nature)
Violation of condition of being under probation
Abandoning a pet inside the University
Major traffic violations
Commission of third minor offense
Tampering with any official school document
False declaration of information on student's admission credentials
Using someone else's ID card or COR; lending ID card or COR to someone else or to an outsider
Giving false statements during investigation or official inquiry to secure a favorable decision or avoid liability
Stealing money or property, or any attempt thereof
Acts of gross dishonesty
Cheating in any form
Plagiarism
Violation of any of the provisions enshrined in the non-fraternity/sorority agreement
Persuading students to join an organization not recognized by the University
Engaging in any of the acts prohibited in RA 8049 (Anti-Hazing Law)
Threatening to attack or assault a person or their honor inside campus or within the immediate vicinity — verbally, in writing, or through electronic means; or during approved off-campus activity

Brawls and/or physically attacking or assaulting any person inside or outside campus, within the immediate vicinity of the University, or during official off-campus activities — whether or not resulting in physical injuries
Improper Uniform / Dress Code Violation
Inappropriate blowing of horns
